data.title: Atkinsréalis, Bombardier, Kinaxis
data.description:
Securities analysts revised their ratings and price targets on several Canadian companies, including Atkinsréalis, Bombardier and Kinaxis on Friday.

HIGHLIGHTS

  • * Atkinsréalis : Scotiabank raises target price to C$113 from C$108

  • * Bombardier : JP Morgan raises target price to C$170 from C$135

  • * Kinaxis Inc : Scotiabank raises target price to C$240 from C$225

Following is a summary of research actions on Canadian companies reported by Reuters on Friday. Stock entries are in alphabetical order.

  • * Atkinsréalis : Scotiabank raises target price to C$113 from C$108

  • * Bombardier : JP Morgan raises target price to C$170 from C$135

  • * Canadian Tire Corporation Ltd : TD Cowen cuts target price to C$184 from C$201

  • * Kinaxis Inc : RBC raises target price to C$235 from C$225

  • * Kinaxis Inc : Scotiabank raises target price to C$240 from C$225

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0737 GMT - DBS's growth should be sustainable in the medium term, Maybank IB's Thilan Wickramasinghe says in a note. The Singapore lender generated above-market-trend growth across all pillars in 2Q, which he expects to be supported by DBS's scale and strong management execution. DBS says it can raise base dividends while rolling out its capital returns program until 2027, which provides yield clarity, he adds. He expects DBS's early deployment of artificial intelligence and new verticals such as digital assets to further consolidate its lead over rivals. He raises his 2025-2027 earnings forecasts by 5%-11%, based on his more positive operational assumptions. Maybank IB lifts its rating to buy from hold and raises its target to S$56.15 from S$45.26. Shares are 1.4% higher at S$50.45. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

0725 GMT - Jungheinrich flagged a continued subdued environment in its core European markets and heightened competition in its second-quarter results, which suggest a recently launched restructuring was a good decision, Jefferies analysts say. "We see the restructuring as the right strategic actions given limited growth in Europe and increased competitive pressure, with the majority of effects seen in 2027," the analysts write in a research note. The German forklift maker reported second-quarter results that were slightly better than it indicated when it cut its outlook twice last month, Jefferies says. Orders returned to growth as expected despite weakness in Europe, but cash flow was on the weaker side, the analysts say. Shares fall 3.1%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0724 GMT - European luxury companies could start to see a rebound after weak results, Deutsche Bank analysts write in a research note. The second-quarter earnings season didn't throw up many surprises, with most luxury brands seeing a sequential slowdown from the previous three months, the analysts say. Companies that are more exposed to tourism and to lower-income consumers faced greater challenges, they add. Despite current difficulties, Deutsche Bank expects a sequential sales improvement in the second half. "The investor view on the sector remains largely negative but there are early signs of this starting to change," DB says. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - NN Group's first-half solvency 2 ratio of 208% will excite investors, KBW analyst Michele Ballatore writes. The Dutch insurance and asset-management group's solvency 2 result solidly beat expectations, he adds. In the absence of any material shocks in the second half of the year, an upgraded share buyback could be closer than expected, he adds. Elsewhere, NN Group's operating capital generation beat consensus views by 7%, driven by a better-than-expected performance in its NL Life division, he adds. Shares trade up 1% at 61.46 euros. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0717 GMT - Airbus delivered 373 commercial aircraft in the first seven months of the year, down 7% on year, a figure that is still far from its full-year target of 820 aircraft, RBC Europe analysts say. To meet this target, the European planemaker will need to deliver an average of 89 aircraft a month until December, they say. The group ended the second quarter with 60 A320 gliders--aircraft without engines. Assuming it has delivered those by year-end, then Airbus would need to deliver 77 aircraft a month to meet the full-year target of 820, the analysts add. This monthly amount is achievable for Airbus, they say. Shares are down 0.1% at 177.18 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0706 GMT - Airbus' 67 aircraft deliveries in July exceeded latest press expectations, Deutsche Bank's analyst Christophe Menard says. The European planemaker increased its deliveries of the A320 aircraft family to 54 units last month, in what Menard calls a reassuring recovery. Airbus only received three net orders in July, after a customer canceled one for an A350-F aircraft, Menard notes. Deutsche Bank raises its target price to 186 euros from 185 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0706 GMT - Airbus announced 67 airliner deliveries in July, bringing the year-to-date total to 373, which is still behind the average, Citi says in a note to investors. However, this does represent a slight catch-up versus June deliveries, it says. Airbus also had 60 so-called gliders--aircraft built but awaiting engines--below Citi's estimate of 65 gliders at the end of July, it adds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0705 GMT - Munich Re's 5% cut to its revenue guidance for the year will lead to a 2% drop in consensus estimates, RBC Capital Markets says in a note. The German reinsurer now expects its insurance revenue to come in at 62 billion euros, against 64 billion euros previously due to a lower expected result in its reinsurance division on foreign exchange fluctuations. Elsewhere, Munich Re backed its 6 billion euro net profit guidance after it confirmed its prereleased second-quarter net profit beat. It flagged strong earnings momentum across all business segments, which has translated into high consensus expectations, analyst Mandeep Jagpal and associate Sarah Chong write. "The stock has been very strong coming into these results, up 8% over the last week, partly due to sector rotation, we believe," they add. Shares fall 6%. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0623 GMT - KT&G appears to have room to improve shareholder returns, Nomura analyst Siwoo Kim writes in a note. The South Korean tobacco company plans to monetize non-core assets for additional share buybacks and aggressive dividend payouts, Kim says. The company is seen as offsetting sluggishness in the domestic tobacco market with higher average selling prices and bigger volumes for products in overseas markets, he notes. New factories in Kazakhstan and Indonesia could help KT&G improve its profit margin on lower production costs, he adds. Nomura upgrades its rating on the stock to buy from neutral and raises its target price by 38% to 161,000 won. Shares are 4.8% higher at 145,100 won. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0615 GMT - Riverstone Holdings retains a bull in RHB Research which sees the gloves manufacturer in a "sweet spot." The company is benefiting from a robust pickup in global semiconductor sales in 2025, which is estimated to grow 15.4%, according to World Semiconductor Trade Statistics organization. Cleanroom gloves manufactured by companies such as Riverstone are essential in semiconductor chip manufacturing to prevent contamination and ensure product quality. However, RHB cuts its earnings forecasts for Riverstone by 13% for 2025, 12% for 2026, and 13% for 2027 after the company's 2Q results were below expectations. It trims the stock's target price to S$0.85 from S$0.95 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 4.8% lower at S$0.69. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0610 GMT - Shenzhou International Group will likely absorb just 2 to 3 percentage points of the U.S. tariff burden, Morningstar analyst Ivan Su writes in a note. He says the new 20% tariff on Vietnamese imports is unlikely to heavily affect the earnings outlook of the Chinese apparel maker, which has factories in Vietnam. Only about 16% of Shenzhou's revenue comes from the U.S., and both its revenue and net profit are on track for double-digit growth this year, he says. Despite softer demand from Nike, Shenzhou continues to receive strong orders from Adidas, Uniqlo and Puma, Morningstar adds. The brokerage maintains its HK$110.00 fair-value estimate on the company. Shares are last at HK$56.45. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0606 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.2% at around 2599. President Trump nominated economic council head Stephen Miran as a temporary member of the Federal Reserve's Board of Governors. Fed member Christopher Waller appears to be the clear favorite to succeed Fed Chair Powell next year, SEB senior economist Johan Javeus writes. "On geopolitics, we note that Trump says he can meet Putin next week, even though Putin has not yet agreed to allow Zelenskiy to participate." The Nikkei is sharply higher after good reports in the tech sector and hopes of easing trade tensions with the U.S., while Hong Kong is lower and Shanghai flat, Javeus says. Equity futures are pointing higher in Europe and the U.S. OMXS30 closed at 2593.76, OMXN40 at 2219.01 and OBX at 1537. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

The Singapore lender generated above-market-trend growth across all pillars in 2Q, which he expects to be supported by DBS's scale and strong management execution. DBS says it can raise base dividends while rolling out its capital returns program until 2027, which provides yield clarity, he adds. data.title: Austria Industrial Output Rises in June
data.description:
Industrial production in Austria grew by 0.6% year-on-year in June 2025, following an upwardly revised 1.1% increase in the previous month.

Production grew significantly for consumer non-durables (6.3% vs 3.1% in May) and intermediate goods (6.1% vs 2.2%), while capital goods rebounded (0.6% vs -0.7%).

Additionally, production fell at a softer pace for consumer durables (-9.6% vs -11%).

Meanwhile, output declined further for energy (-7.9% vs -4.7%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output fell by 0.2% in June, easing from a downwardly revised 0.4% decline in the preceding period.

data.title: PRESSR: Bain & Company releases brief on the future of financial planning
data.description:
Middle East. Bain & Company has released a brief, The Future of Financial Planning Is Autonomous, which explores how generative AI and intelligent agents are transforming the way companies plan, forecast, and allocate capital.

According to the brief, traditional budgeting cycles are too slow and rigid for today’s volatile markets. AI-native agents embedded in enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems are enabling companies to update forecasts continuously, adjust budgets automatically, and deliver real-time strategic insights to leadership teams. These systems mark the beginning of a new era of intelligent, autonomous finance.

More than 25% of finance teams now use some form of machine learning in quarterly planning, with adoption rates for generative AI accelerating rapidly. Generative AI enables faster and more accurate forecasting by synthesizing signals from unstructured data and providing scenario-based insights. Agentic AI takes it a step further by autonomously managing end-to-end forecasting workflows.

The brief also details how leading organizations like Microsoft are embedding AI agents into core FP&A functions, from forecasting and variance analysis to reconciliation and reporting, significantly reducing cycle times and boosting responsiveness.

Bain outlines three clear paths to modernizing planning: streamlining existing processes, enhancing them with AI, or fully reinventing the operating model. Companies like Eaton and Hilti are profiled as early leaders who have embraced integrated, agile planning models, delivering both speed and strategic agility.

About Bain & Company

Bain & Company is a global consultancy that helps the world’s most ambitious change makers define the future. Across 65 cities in 40 countries, we work alongside our clients as one team with a shared ambition to achieve extraordinary results, outperform the competition, and redefine industries. We complement our tailored, integrated expertise with a vibrant ecosystem of digital innovators to deliver better, faster, and more enduring outcomes. Our 10-year commitment to invest more than $1 billion in pro bono services brings our talent, expertise, and insight to organizations tackling today’s urgent challenges in education, racial equity, social justice, economic development, and the environment.

We earned a platinum rating from EcoVadis, the leading platform for environmental, social, and ethical performance ratings for global supply chains, putting us in the top 1% of all companies. Since our founding in 1973, we have measured our success by the success of our clients, and we proudly maintain the highest level of client advocacy in the industry.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Seychelles Inflation Rate Highest in 7 Months
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Seychelles rose to 0.64% in July 2025, marking the highest level since January and up from 0.48% in the previous month.

The acceleration was largely driven by higher inflation in non-food items, which increased to 0.44% from 0.28% in June.

In contrast, price growth slowed for other food items, easing to 2.21% from 2.34%.

Meanwhile, fish prices continued to decline, though at a slower pace, registering a 1.33% drop compared to a 4.64% fall in June.

Core inflation also picked up, reaching 0.65%—its highest level since January 2023—up from 0.52% in the previous month.

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.17%, rebounding from a 0.07% decline in June.

data.title: Roche Could Reach Peak Earnings Growth This Year — Market Talk
data.description: Roche Holding finds itself in a tough spot, with earnings growth likely to peak this year, lack of pipeline momentum and a pivot into obesity at risk from market uncertainty and competition, Morgan Stanley analysts say. "We believe consensus expectations may be too optimistic given potential branded and biosimilar competition to the base business, which will likely increase over 2027-2029," they write in a research note. Trial results the Swiss pharma giant is due to release in the fourth quarter seem high-risk and 2026 looks set to be another light year for its drug pipeline, the analysts say. Morgan Stanley cuts its recommendation on the stock to underweight from equalweight, and trims its target price to 245 Swiss francs from 286 francs. Shares fall 0.8% to 247.10 francs. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: GSK to Receive $370 Million in U.S. Patent Litigation Settlement
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

GSK said it would receive $370 million as part of a U.S. patent settlement between CureVac and BioNTech related to messenger mRNA-based Covid-19 vaccines.

The U.K. pharmaceutical giant said Friday that, under the terms of an existing license agreement with CureVac, it would receive an upfront payment of $320 million in cash, with the rest of the money through an amendment to the deal with CureVac. It will also receive a 1% royalty on future U.S. sales of influenza, Covid-19 and other related combination mRNA vaccine products made by BioNTech and Pfizer.

The payment will be recorded as other operating income in GSK's third-quarter financial results, it said.

Biotech company CureVac said Thursday that it had reached a series of agreements with BioNTech and Pfizer to resolve all pending patent litigation in the U.S. with GSK related to mRNA-based Covid-19 vaccines. Under the agreements both it and GSK would receive a payment of $740 million, CureVac said.

GSK said the settlement didn't affect the enforcement of its own patents against Pfizer and BioNTech in the U.S. or Europe.

"GSK will continue with its litigation against BioNTech and Pfizer for infringement of GSK's patents", it said.

Pfizer didn't immediately respond to a request for comment. BioNTech, which denies the allegations raised by CureVac or GSK, said the settlement didn't represent an admission of liability.

London-listed GSK shares in early trade were up 22.50 pence, or 1.6%, at 1,405.50 pence.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

India's Venky's hits over 2-year low after Q1 profit plunge

** Shares of Venky's India falls 2.2% to 1456.6 rupees, their lowest since April 2023

** Poultry co's Q1 profit sinks 79%, hurt by a spike in expenses; rev up 7%

** More than 29,000 shares traded, over 1.6x the 30-day avg

** Stock down 20% YTD

data.title: Turkish Galata Wind Enerji up after Q2 profit beat
data.description:
** Shares in Galata Wind Enerji rise around 3% after Turkey-based renewable energy company, specializing in the generation of electrical energy from wind and solar power sources, reported better-than-expected Q2 net profit

** The company announced Q2 net profit of 270.5 million lira ($6.65 million) versus net profit of 257.0 million lira year ago

** Brokerage Global Menkul Degerler says it Galata Wind's Q2 net profit came in 47% above market expectations

** It adds that higher financing costs and lower monetary gains weighed on results, while lower tax expenses provided support

** "Galata Wind's stronger-than-expected Q2 net profit could have a positive impact on the stock" Global Menkul Degerler notes

($1 = 40.6758 liras)

data.title: U.S. Futures Up, European Stocks Mixed as Markets Digest Trump's New Tariffs
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.2% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.2%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 climbed 0.2% in morning trading. Bechtle added 10.6% and Lotus Bakeries rose 7%. On the other hand, TBC Bank Group lost 11.1%, and Muenchener Rueckversicher fell 7.2%. The FTSE 100 added 0.2%. Other stocks in Europe were mixed as France's CAC 40 increased 0.3% and Germany's DAX dropped 0.2%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index climbed 0.1% to 95.33.

In commodities, Brent crude dropped 0.2% to $66.33 a barrel, and WTI crude fell 0.2% to $63.73 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was down 0.3% to 32.86 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield climbed by 2 basis points to 2.649% from 2.633%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury held steady at 4.257%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index gained 1.9%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 1%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite dropped 0.1%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

7.             If details included in a disclosure under Rule 8 are incorrect, they should be corrected as soon as practicable in a subsequent disclosure. Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rule 8 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to "the Rules" are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.

data.title: U.S. Battery Startup Lyten Buys Northvolt's Remaining European Assets
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

U.S. battery startup Lyten has agreed to buy the remaining European assets of bankrupt Swedish battery maker Northvolt.

Northvolt filed for bankruptcy in March after it failed to secure the financial backing needed to continue operating, but the bankruptcy trustee revealed in June that three possible buyers had been found, including a foreign investor that had bid to take over the entire operation.

Northvolt has continued to operate since its bankruptcy, but on a scaled-down basis, after the bankruptcy trustee reached an agreement in principle with key stakeholders earlier this year. Parts of the business have already been sold off, including its heavy industry business to Scania and a Swedish battery plant to joint venture partner Volvo Car.

Lyten, a Silicon Valley startup developing lithium-sulfur batteries which are seen as more environmentally friendly than lithium-ion batteries, is backed by auto giant Stellantis, logistics provider FedEx and industrial conglomerate Honeywell, among others.

It said late Thursday it has agreed to buy Northvolt's main factory and research and development facility which are both located in Sweden, as well as the site of a future so-called gigafactory in Germany and all intellectual property.

The price Lyten has paid wasn't disclosed, but it confirmed the acquisition includes assets valued at around $5 billion.

"Lyten's mission is to be the leading supplier of clean, locally sourced and manufactured batteries and energy storage systems in both North America and Europe," Lyten Chief Executive and Co-Founder Dan Cook said in a statement.

"The acquisition of Northvolt's assets brings the facilities and Swedish talent to accelerate this mission by years."

The company plans to rehire a significant portion of the previously laid-off workforce and will assess staffing needs site by site as it restarts and scales operations.

Northvolt had a workforce of around 5,500 when it filed for bankruptcy and the previous deal to continue operating on a scaled-down basis meant around 1,700 workers stayed on in Sweden.

Lyten expects to immediately restart operations at the Swedish sites upon closing and is working with the German government to continue developing the site there. Talks with Canadian officials are also continuing, as it pursues the acquisition of Northvolt's North American gigafactory under construction in Quebec.

The startup had previously announced the acquisition of Northvolt's battery manufacturing facility in California, its battery energy storage system manufacturing facility in Poland and its battery energy storage system product and intellectual property portfolio.

Founded in 2015, Lyten has received more than $625 million in equity investment and secured letters of intent for $650 million in financing from the Export Import Bank of the U.S.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Helping Beverage Companies Navigate The EUDR: Beverage Industry Environmental Roundtable (BIER) Releases Sector-Specific Interpretation Guide
data.description:
NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / August 8, 2025 / The Beverage Industry Environmental Roundtable (BIER) has released the Beverage Industry EUDR Interpretation Guide, a practical, sector-specific resource designed to help beverage companies understand and meet the requirements of the European Union Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) (EU 2023/1115).

Effective June 29, 2023, the EUDR introduced strict due diligence requirements for companies placing cattle, coffee, cocoa, soy, palm oil, rubber, or wood - or products derived from them - on the EU market or exporting them from it.

For the beverage industry, this regulation is highly relevant as it covers both raw ingredients (like coffee, cocoa, and palm derivatives) and select wood-based packaging (such as barrels and branded wooden crates). Full compliance for large and medium-sized operators begins December 30, 2025, with Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs) following by June 30, 2026.

The guide is designed for beverage manufacturers, brand owners, and procurement and sustainability teams who need clear, actionable direction on how to implement EUDR compliance across their supply chains. It provides:

  • Sector-specific guidance on identifying which ingredients and materials fall within the scope of the EUDR

  • Step-by-step instructions for mapping supply chains, assessing and mitigating risk, and maintaining traceability

  • Support for compliance workflows, including preparing and submitting Due Diligence Statements (DDS) via the TRAde Control and Expert System (TRACES), i.e., the EU's official digital platform for EUDR implementation

  • Integration strategies to align EUDR obligations with broader sustainability initiatives like no-deforestation commitments and SBTi FLAG goals

By providing a beverage-focused lens on a complex regulation, the guide empowers companies to reduce compliance risk, maintain market access, and build robust, future-proof sustainability practices.

"The EUDR presents both a challenge and an opportunity for the beverage sector. Our members needed clear, actionable guidance on how to navigate this complex regulation, and that's exactly what this guide delivers, helping companies meet their obligations while advancing environmental stewardship." ~ Erica Pann, Executive Director of BIER.

The Beverage Industry EUDR Interpretation Guide reinforces BIER's mission to accelerate environmental progress in the beverage sector by turning complex sustainability challenges into actionable solutions.

The Beverage Industry EUDR Interpretation Guide is available for download at https://www.bieroundtable.com/publication/beverage-industry-eudr-interpretation-guide/

For more information, please contact: Erica Pann, BIER Executive Director

Erica.Pann@anteagroup.us

About BIER

BIER is a technical coalition of leading global beverage companies working together to advance environmental sustainability within the beverage sector. Formed in 2006, BIER is a common voice across the beverage sector, speaking to influence global standards on environmental sustainability aspects most relevant to the sector, affect change both up and down the supply chain, and share best practices that raise the bar for environmental performance of the industry. By doing so, BIER is able to monitor data and trends, engage with key stakeholders, develop best practices, and guide a course of action for the future. BIER members include Anheuser-Busch InBev, Asahi Group Holdings, Bacardi, Brown-Forman, Carlsberg Group, The Coca-Cola Company, Constellation Brands, Diageo, Heineken, Keurig Dr Pepper, LION, Molson Coors, Monster Energy, Ocean Spray Cranberries, PepsiCo, Pernod Ricard and Suntory Global Spirits. For more information, visit www.bieroundtable.com.

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Beverage Industry Environmental Roundtable on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Beverage Industry Environmental Roundtable

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/beverage-industry-environmental-roundtable

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Beverage Industry Environmental Roundtable

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: India stocks, rupee, bonds swaps, call at 1 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.53% at 80,199, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index dipped 0.54% to 24,464, dragged by declines in information technology and pharmaceutical stocks, and as investor sentiment remained sour due to India-U.S. trade tensions.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee was steady versus the U.S. dollar at 87.70, as the initial rally was capped by dollar demand from importers and short-term speculators, who were wary of the overhang from U.S. trade tensions.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN063335G=CC) was quoted at 99.6025 rupees, with the yield steady at 6.3837%, as investors awaited the results of weekly debt auction that will test demand in the wake of a disappointing central bank policy decision.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was little changed at 5.4875%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) dipped 2 basis points to 5.65%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.15% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.48%.

data.title: IXIC: Nasdaq Composite Logs New Record Close Despite Pullbacks in Meta, Microsoft
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nasdaq index powers higher
  • Stocks fall across the board
  • Still, winning week on the cards

Tech-heavy index powered through a session slump and not even deep declines in two tech giants could stop it from settling at a new all-time high.

📈 Nasdaq Defies Broader Retreat

  • The Nasdaq Composite added 0.4% Thursday to close at a record 21,242.70, marking yet another milestone in what’s been a dominant week for the tech-heavy benchmark.
  • It overcame pullbacks over at the Mag 7 corner with Meta wiping out 1.3% and Microsoft shedding 0.8%.
  • The S&P 500 slipped 0.1% to finish the cash session at 6,339.99, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 224 points, or 0.5%, to 43,969.12, after flipping between gains and losses during regular trading.
  • Sector flows told the story: utilities, materials, and consumer staples saw steady inflows, while health care and financials took a breather, with bank stocks particularly sluggish after a week of volatile rate expectations.

🍏 Apple Drops the $100 Billion Extra

  • Apple stock surged 3%, following a 5% Wednesday pop, after announcing plans to funnel an additional $100 billion into US manufacturing over the next four years.
  • That’s on top of a $500 billion pledge in February, bringing the total US investment commitment to $600 billion — a move widely seen as both a supply-chain security play and a political crowd-pleaser (mostly one person, not a full crowd).
  • The spending will include expanding partnerships with component makers like Corning , with Apple noting that “every iPhone and Apple Watch sold around the world” will soon feature Kentucky-made cover glass.

🌍 Tariffs Fade into the Background

  • The White House confirmed that starting Thursday, new tariffs were rolling out on nations without trade pacts with the US, but Wall Street mostly shrugged. Traders have been quick to price in geopolitical noise lately, especially when earnings season is still delivering strong beats.
  • Over at the forex corner, the US dollar gave up some of its weekly gains Friday morning with the euro adding a few pips against the greenback. The sterling was also higher after the Bank of England on Thursday cut interest rates for the fifth time in a year.
  • Still, the week’s scoreboard looks solid: Nasdaq up 2.9%, S&P 500 up 1.6%, and Dow up 0.9% heading into Friday. If the momentum holds, this could mark the Nasdaq’s best week since late June.

data.title: European shares on track for biggest weekly gain in 12; Novo Nordisk rises
data.description:
European shares edged higher and were on track for their biggest weekly gain in 12 as investors assessed U.S. President Donald Trump's nomination for Federal Reserve's open spot on its governing board.

Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk added 3.2% and ranked among the top performers on the Stoxx 600 after rival Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill's late-stage study showed that it lagged Novo's injectable obesity treatment Wegovy.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 0.2%, as of 0715 GMT. Most regional bourses were also trading in the green with Spain's benchmark index IBEX up 0.4%.

UK's blue-chip FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 added 0.2%, a day after the Bank of England cut interest rates by a quarter percentage point in a narrow 5-4 vote with inflation concerns weighing on the decision.

Munich Re shares fell 7% and were among the top decliners on the index after the German reinsurer trimmed its fiscal-year outlook for insurance revenue due to business and foreign exchange rates.

RTL Group shares added 3.1% after the European broadcaster reported its half-year results and confirmed its full-year revenue outlook.

The world's largest online betting company Flutter raised its full-year profit growth forecast but shares fell 1.4%.

Italian finance group Unipol shares fell 2.7% despite reporting a rise in first-half profit.

Trump said he will nominate Council of Economic Advisers Chairman Stephen Miran to serve out the final few months of a newly vacant seat at the Fed while the administration looks for a permanent addition.
